body: # subtitle: An optional subheading for your content.
# date: Set to an ISO-8601 date for when this article was first published.
# lastModified: Set to an ISO-8601 date for when this article was last changed. If this is not present, the published date will be used instead.
# tags: A comma-separated list of tags for this article. If you have enabled tag pages, these will be used to list matching content.
# Comment out until website supports tags
# published: Set to false to leave this article unpublished on your site.
# image: The main image for this content. Will be used with content previews and social sharing.
# imageDescription: Screenreader-friendly text describing your article’s main image.
# layout: The name of the ArticlePage struct in your site to use for this content.
# as the predefined fields, you can use the metadata dictionary to access any custom properties you have defined in the front matter.
# Note that the dictionary values are optionals: your page code must be able to deal with the dictionary item not existing!
Quick, engaging overview to hook viewers.
Brief mention if needed, sometimes skipped.
Depends on complexity of environment setup.
Core part explaining theory or syntax.
Hands-on coding with commentary, often the longest section.
Optional, often integrated within code demonstration.
Not typically part of video runtime, but linked externally.
